title: malus dasyphylla f. pendula
intro: Malus dasyphylla f. pendula, also known as the Chinese flowering crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Malus dasyphylla f. pendula include reducing fever, relieving cough, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, infusion, and tincture, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
